output.final_deliverable: Starting search for primary sources, contemporary news, and modern analyses of the 1932 'Great Emu War' in Australia.
output.action_summary: Running web searches for primary documents, contemporary news articles, and modern analyses about the Great Emu War (1932). I will visit the most relevant pages to extract quotes and details, and will include URLs for all sources.

b43ec886-8eec-4f98-8c61-5553b44733ae.title:
Research the 'Great Emu War' of Australia. Find primary source documents or historical records, modern analysis of the event, and quote at least one passage from a historical document or contemporary news article about it. Provide URLs to all sources.

Benchmark rule: when external evidence is needed, run search then visit the best URLs. Visit pages to extract concrete details and cite visited URLs in the final answer.

details.mandate:
Research the 'Great Emu War' of Australia. Find primary source documents or historical records, modern analysis of the event, and quote at least one passage from a historical document or contemporary news article about it. Provide URLs to all sources.

Benchmark rule: when external evidence is needed, run search then visit the best URLs. Visit pages to extract concrete details and cite visited URLs in the final answer.

f9a9baf5-06a9-4c98-8c1b-6f7fb0e5351c.title: Sequential research workflow: locate primary sources (contemporary newspapers/official records), find modern analyses, extract at least one quoted passage and collect URLs

details.overview: A sequential plan to (1) find primary-source contemporary newspaper reports and any official/military records about the 1932 'Great Emu War', (2) find reputable modern analyses (news outlets, academic or museum pages), (3) visit the best URLs to extract concrete details and at least one direct quoted passage from a historical document or contemporary news article, and (4) produce a short verified summary with all source URLs.
target_facts_to_extract: Event dates and locations (where/when the operations occurred)
Participants and units involved (e.g., soldiers, weapons used such as Lewis guns)
Numbers claimed killed or engagement results (emu casualties, farmer losses)
Official/press language describing the operations (direct quote(s) from 1932 newspapers or official records)
Modern interpretation / analysis of causes and outcome (links and summary)

measurable_output_fields.primary_sources: Array of {title, publication, date, URL, excerpt/quote (>=1)}
measurable_output_fields.official_records: Array of {record title, archive (NAA/AWM/etc.), date, URL, excerpt/quote if available}
measurable_output_fields.modern_analyses: Array of {title, outlet/author, date, URL, short summary (2-3 lines)}
measurable_output_fields.summary: 2-4 sentence verified summary of the event with citations to visited URLs

queries: emu campaign 1932 newspaper Campion Walgoolan 'emu' 'soldiers' 'Lewis gun'
Emu War 'Campion' '1932' 'The West Australian' 'Sydney Morning Herald'
Great Emu War National Archives Australia
emu operations 1932 military report 'Lewis gun' 'Campion' 'Westralian' 'Department of Defence'
Great Emu War academic paper 'emus' 'farmers' 'Western Australia' 1932

verification_steps: For every quoted passage, visit the original scanned newspaper page (Trove) or official archive page and copy the exact text and the URL to the scanned page.
Cross-check reported dates/numbers across at least two independent sources (one primary contemporary report + one modern analysis or another contemporary source).
Prefer museum/archives (NAA/AWM) or major news outlets for modern analysis to avoid relying solely on brief blogs.
details.extraction_intent_for_each_visit: From each visited URL extract: title, author (if present), publication name, date, exact quoted passage(s) to be used (with surrounding context), and the canonical page URL. Tag each source as 'primary' (contemporary 1932 press/official records) or 'secondary' (modern analysis).
details.reduction_of_uncertainty_reason: This sequential approach first secures primary contemporary evidence (reducing uncertainty about what contemporaries wrote), then collects official records (verifying military/administrative involvement), and finally contrasts those with modern analyses to ensure correct interpretation and to provide authoritative URLs and at least one directly quoted passage.
planned_sequence_of_actions: 1) Search (web + Trove) for 'Great Emu War 1932' and identify top primary sources (Trove scanned newspapers) and archive entries (NAA/AWM).
2) Visit (open) the selected Trove newspaper pages and archive records to extract exact quotes and metadata (publication, date, page).
3) Search for reputable modern analyses (BBC, Smithsonian, Australian Geographic, scholarly articles) and visit top matches to extract summaries and URLs.
4) Compile results: list of primary sources with quotes, list of official records (if found), list of modern analyses, and a concise summary with citations (URLs).

min_expected_results.primary_sources_to_return: At least 2 contemporary newspaper articles or a primary scanned page from 1932 with one direct quote extracted
min_expected_results.official_records_to_return: At least 1 archival entry or government/military reference if available (NAA/AWM)
min_expected_results.modern_analyses_to_return: At least 2 reputable modern analyses with URLs
min_expected_results.summary: A short (2-4 sentence) summary citing 3-5 URLs
